issues.message: The objective 'Use Matplotlib for plot structure, geometry control, and basic charting' mentions 'geometry control,' but the worked examples primarily focus on basic charting (line, scatter) and labels/titles. More advanced geometry control (e.g., figure size, subplots, axis limits) is not explicitly demonstrated, which might leave learners unable to fully achieve this part of the objective.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a worked example or expand an existing one to explicitly demonstrate aspects of Matplotlib's geometry control, such as setting figure size, creating subplots, or adjusting axis limits.

issues.message: The estimated time of 30 minutes seems ambitious for a beginner to fully absorb the core explanation, understand all four worked examples, and then successfully complete three practice tasks. Some practice tasks introduce new plot types (bar chart, box plot) or more advanced customization (legend with two lines) not explicitly covered in the worked examples, requiring additional learning time.
issues.suggested_fix: Increase the `estimated_time_minutes` to 45-60 minutes to allow sufficient time for practice and exploration. Alternatively, add more explicit guidance/hints for the practice tasks, or include worked examples for bar charts and multi-line plots to better prepare learners.
